steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content:
The researchers claim that juvenile plants gain an advantage by growing near other plants.

Since they established that random distribution would produce only 15% of plants in patches, supporting their claim means showing that actual percentages of juvenile plants in patches exceed this 15% baseline.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D directly states that for each species, the percentage of juveniles in patches was substantially higher than the 15% expected under random distribution.

This demonstrates the claimed proximity advantage by showing juveniles benefit from growing in patches.

The other choices either ignore the 15% baseline comparison, name only a single species, or claim percentages fall below the baseline—contradicting the claim of advantage.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
